mission.title: Native JSON Authoring Pilot — dark mode & smartphone battery
mission.decision_supported: Whether NH should mention dark mode as a battery-saving measure in an internal device-usage note.
research_question: Does enabling dark mode meaningfully reduce smartphone battery consumption, and under what conditions?
mission.scope: Consumer smartphones; OLED/AMOLED vs LCD; public technical measurements ~2018-2024.
mission.out_of_scope: Laptops, desktops, tablets; proprietary battery telemetry; eye-strain/health effects.
mission.reviewer: Founder (author-reviewer; L2 — independent reviewer preferred, not required, per NH-ROP §18)

claims.statement: On OLED/AMOLED smartphone displays, dark mode reduces display power draw because black pixels emit little to no light.
claims.confidence_rationale: Established OLED display physics plus a direct independent measurement study (Purdue 2021); vendor documentation concurs. Verdict kept at Supported (not Confirmed) because primary access was secondary-only.
claims.overturn_conditions: A credible measurement showing OLED black pixels draw non-trivial power.
claims.origin: Purdue 2021 study; general OLED display physics.

claims.statement: At ~100% screen brightness, switching to dark mode saves roughly 39-47% of OLED display power on the tested phones.
claims.scope: OLED phones at ~100% brightness; Purdue 2021 test set (Pixel 2, Moto Z3, Pixel 4, Pixel 5).
claims.confidence_rationale: Single independent measurement study (Purdue 2021); not yet independently replicated across other vendors/devices.
claims.overturn_conditions: An independent measurement materially contradicting the 39-47% range.

claims.statement: At typical indoor brightness (~30-50%), switching to dark mode saves only about 3-9% of OLED display power on the tested phones.
claims.scope: OLED phones at 30-50% brightness; Purdue 2021 test set.
claims.confidence_rationale: Single independent measurement study; consistent with the exponential brightness-to-power relationship the same study reports.
claims.overturn_conditions: An independent measurement materially contradicting the 3-9% range.

claims.statement: On LCD smartphone displays, dark mode yields negligible battery savings because the backlight stays on regardless of pixel colour.
claims.confidence_rationale: Follows from LCD backlight physics and is widely corroborated in technical explainers; no per-device measurement is cited here.
claims.overturn_conditions: A measurement showing material dark-mode battery savings on an LCD phone.

claims.statement: The unqualified, condition-independent claim 'dark mode saves battery' is over-general.
claims.scope: The general/marketing claim across all phones and all conditions.
claims.confidence_rationale: Savings are demonstrably conditional on OLED hardware and high brightness, so a universal phrasing holds only in part.
claims.overturn_conditions: Evidence that dark mode saves material battery across display types and brightness levels.
claims.origin: Contrast between popular framing and the Purdue 2021 measurement.

claims.statement: Some vendor and popular-tech framing presents dark mode as a broad battery saver without stating the OLED/brightness conditions.
claims.scope: Vendor and popular-tech communications about dark mode.
claims.confidence_rationale: Such framing is directly observable in vendor and popular sources; it establishes reported framing, not technical truth.
claims.overturn_conditions: Evidence that vendor/popular framing consistently states the OLED/brightness conditions.

claims.statement: Dark mode meaningfully extends total device battery runtime (not just display power) under typical mixed use.
claims.scope: Whole-device battery runtime under typical mixed use.
claims.confidence_rationale: Cited sources isolate display power, not whole-device runtime; the typical-brightness display saving (3-9%) is only a fraction of total device draw and was not measured end-to-end.
claims.overturn_conditions: A controlled whole-device runtime measurement isolating the dark-mode effect under typical use.
claims.origin: Gap between display-power measurements and whole-device runtime.

evidence.exact_finding: On OLED phones, switching to dark mode reduces display power draw; magnitude is brightness-dependent.
evidence.extraction_method: web search of Purdue newsroom summary
evidence.quote_or_paraphrase: dark mode reduces OLED display power; savings depend on brightness
evidence.applicable_scope: OLED phones (Pixel 2, Moto Z3, Pixel 4, Pixel 5)
evidence.limitations: reached via secondary summary; primary paper not fetched
evidence.researcher_notes: MD trace: RESEARCH_RECORD.md Evidence table row M005-C1-E1

evidence.exact_finding: Vendor documentation states dark theme saves power on OLED/AMOLED displays.
evidence.quote_or_paraphrase: dark theme reduces power on OLED

evidence.exact_finding: At ~100% brightness, dark mode saves an average of 39-47% of OLED display power.
evidence.applicable_scope: OLED phones at ~100% brightness; Purdue test set

evidence.exact_finding: At 30-50% brightness, dark mode saves only ~3-9% of OLED display power on average.
evidence.applicable_scope: OLED phones at 30-50% brightness; Purdue test set

evidence.exact_finding: On LCD, the backlight stays on regardless of pixel colour, so dark mode barely affects battery.
evidence.quote_or_paraphrase: LCD backlight always on -> dark mode negligible on LCD
evidence.limitations: explainer, not a device measurement; corroborated by display physics

evidence.exact_finding: Savings are conditional on OLED hardware and high brightness; at typical brightness they are small.
evidence.quote_or_paraphrase: may not save as much as you think; conditional on brightness/hardware

evidence.exact_finding: Vendor and popular framing presents dark mode as a battery saver, often without stating conditions.
evidence.quote_or_paraphrase: dark mode saves battery (conditions often omitted)

sources.title: Purdue University — 'Dark mode may not save your phone's battery life as much as you think' (release on the OLED dark-mode measurement study)
sources.reliability_notes: institutional summary of a peer-reviewed OLED power-measurement study

sources.title: Google / Android dark-theme power guidance
sources.reliability_notes: vendor framing; establishes documentation, not independent measurement

sources.title: Technical explainer: LCD backlight vs OLED for dark-mode battery
sources.reliability_notes: explains LCD backlight physics; corroborated widely

assumptions.statement: The 2021 OLED measurements (Purdue Pixel/Moto set) generalize to current mainstream OLED phones.
assumptions.why_needed: to apply 2021 magnitudes to a present-day recommendation.
assumptions.evidence_status: not independently re-measured in this pilot

unknowns.exact_open_question: What is dark mode's effect on whole-device battery runtime (not display-only power) under typical mixed use?
unknowns.why_it_matters: the decision concerns real-world runtime, not display power alone.
search_attempts: public search of dark-mode battery studies (2026-07-18)
unknowns.missing_evidence: controlled whole-device runtime measurement isolating the dark-mode effect
unknowns.recommended_next_action: find or commission a whole-device runtime test

contradictions.nature_of_conflict: Popular/vendor universal framing (C6) vs measured brightness-conditional magnitude (C2/C3).
contradictions.impact_on_verdict: resolved by qualifying scope to OLED + brightness; supports C5.

verdicts.confidence_rationale: Established OLED physics + independent measurement (secondary access), vendor concurrence.
verdicts.overturn_conditions: OLED black pixels shown to draw non-trivial power.

verdicts.scope_of_validity: OLED at ~100% brightness; Purdue test set.
OLED at 30-50% brightness; Purdue test set.

verdicts.confidence_rationale: LCD backlight physics; widely corroborated; no device measurement here.
Condition-dependence is well established; the universal phrasing holds only in part.
verdicts.overturn_conditions: Material savings across display types and brightness.

verdicts.confidence_rationale: Framing is directly observable; establishes reported framing only.
Whole-device runtime not isolated in cited sources.
